calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Mar-30-2026 Dismissal - No Appearance

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Plaintiff Badarch Ochirsukhs Attendance At Deposition; And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Plaintiff Badarch Ochirsukh
Off Calendar Mar-11-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT HENG LIU'S MOTION TO COMPEL PLAINTIFF BADARCH OCHIRSUKH'S ATTENDANCE AT DEPOSITION; AND REQUEST FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS AGAINST PLAINTIFF BADARCH OCHIRSUKH

No appearances. The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: The unopposed motion to compel the attendance of plaintiff Badarch Ochirsukh to attend a deposition pursuant to a notice served on August 4, 2025, is granted. Ochirsukh shall appear for remote deposition within 10 days of electronic service of this order. The court denies sanctions, finding it would be unjust to award them under all the circumstances of this case. Defendant shall serve notice of this order forthwith in light of the trial date. Order submitted is under review. Judge: Christine Van Aken, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (301/CVA)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Plaintiff Saruul Tseveendees Attendance At Deposition; And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Plaintiff Saruul Tseveendee
Off Calendar Mar-11-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT HENG LIU'S MOTION TO COMPEL PLAINTIFF SARUUL TSEVEENDEE'S ATTENDANCE AT DEPOSITION; AND REQUEST FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS AGAINST PLAINTIFF SARUUL TSEVEENDEE

No appearances. The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: The unopposed motion to compel the attendance of plaintiff Saruul Tseveendees to attend a deposition pursuant to a notice served on August 4, 2025, is granted. Tseveendees shall appear for remote deposition within 10 days of electronic service of this order. The court denies sanctions, finding it would be unjust to award them under all the circumstances of this case. Defendant shall serve notice of this order forthwith in light of the trial date. Order submitted is under review. Judge: Christine Van Aken, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (301/CVA)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Plaintiffs' Discovery Response To Form Interrogatories, Special Interrogatories Demand For Inspection (Sets One); Reqeust For Monetary Sanctions Against Plaintiffs' Counsel Allan M. Schuman, Esq. Of Allan M. Schuman & Associates
Off Calendar Oct-23-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT HENG LIU Motion To Compel Plaintiffs' Discovery Response To Form Interrogatories, Special Interrogatories Demand For Inspection (Sets One); Reqeust For Monetary Sanctions Against Plaintiffs' Counsel Allan M. Schuman, Esq. Of Allan M. Schuman & Associates

Defendant Heng Liu's motion to compel responses to form and special interrogatories and requests for production is granted in part.

Liu served the discovery on plaintiffs, who have not responded by the agreed extended deadline. Liu is entitled to objection-free responses. Plaintiffs Badarch Ochirsukh and Saruul Tseveendee are ordered to respond to the outstanding discovery within 20 days of entry of this order. (See Code Civ. Proc., section 2030.290, subd. (a); id., section 2031.300, subd. (a).)

Liu seeks sanctions. The opposition brief reveals that plaintiffs, who are based in Mongolia, have not responded to communications. By filing a lawsuit, a plaintiff takes on discovery obligations, and failure to maintain contact with counsel is not substantial justification for the lack of responses. (See Code Civ. Proc., section 2030.290, subd. (c); id., section 2031.300, subd. (c).) The court reduces the amount of sanctions in light of all the circumstances. Ochirsukh and Tseveendee are jointly and severally liable for $250 in sanctions to Liu. They are ordered to pay within 30 days of entry of this order.

Proposed order submitted.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/CVA)
